name: Jimmy Slyde
abstract: James Titus Godbolt (October 2, 1927 – May 16, 2008), known professionally as Jimmy Slyde and also as the "King of Slides", was an American tap dancer known for his innovative tap style mixed with jazz. Slyde was a popular rhythm tap dancer in America in the mid-20th century, when he performed on the nightclub and burlesque circuits.

concerts.label: From Harlem to Hollywood: A Tribute to the Nicholas Brothers "Tap Legends"
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: He saw Jimmy Slyde dance, Jimmy Slyde was one of the greatest tap dancers in the world, he died a couple of years ago. And he saw Jimmy Slyde tap dance, he had never seen Jimmy Slyde before, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Bunnett
about_quotes.evidence: I think it might have been Jo Jones. And Jimmy Slyde and some of the great dancers, tap dancers. That was really, what do you call it — a collaboration, a beautiful collaboration.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Bunnett interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 25, 2024
